label: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 21

extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIČ won the stage 21 ITT by 14 seconds over CORT, claiming his third consecutive Vuelta title with a 4:42 margin over MAS.
tactical_patterns: YATES attacked early ITT split times with 29-second advantage over HAIG but faded badly in second half, revealing pacing misjudgment.
ČERNÝ held virtual hot seat for one full hour before CORT displaced him, showing Czech TT champion's strength on rolling courses.
HAIG defended podium by containing YATES' early deficit, demonstrating superior ITT pacing discipline under GC pressure from behind.

rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: Nearly made wrong-way navigation error yet still beat specialist CORT by 14 seconds, showing concentration recovered immediately under pressure.
rider_signals.CORT Magnus: positive: Posted time good enough to hold hot seat despite three prior stage wins suggesting accumulated fatigue negligible.
rider_signals.YATES Adam: negative: Grabbed 29-second early split over HAIG but admitted second half was toughest he remembered, clear pacing collapse pattern.
rider_signals.HAIG Jack: positive: Defended podium despite 29-second early deficit to YATES, superior ITT execution under defensive GC pressure.
rider_signals.MAS Enric: neutral: Finished second overall at 4:42, maintaining GC position without notable ITT performance mentioned.
rider_signals.CARUSO Damiano: positive: Won a stage during this Vuelta, contributing to Bahrain Victorious team classification success.
rider_signals.MÄDER Gino: positive: Secured young riders jersey while helping HAIG to podium, dual classification success indicates climbing strength maintained through week three.
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: neutral: Final professional race appearance, retired mid-stage on own terms with crowd support.
